headline: RELIEF RALLY STARTED — banks +0.7% led by BAHL/UBL, E&P bouncing +1.0%. Local bid only; foreign FIPI still 4d net-sell. Hold positions, do NOT chase banks until FIPI flips positive.
macro_lens: Brent USD 110.44000244140625/bbl (still elevated +20% 21d), KSE-100 closed 162820.41 (-2.29%). Foreign net-sell streak 4d, cumulative -1.94 PKR mn — local mutual fund / retail bid is the only relief source today.

narrative:
TUESDAY MAY 19, 13:25 PKT — RELIEF RALLY HAS STARTED.
Local bid is in; foreign confirmation still pending.

TAPE NOW
- Universe broad bounce: ~+0.5% mid-session
- KSE-100 live: 162646.32  (0.52%)
- BKTI live: 44170.62  (0.67%) — banks leading bounce
- OGTI live: 34319.81  (1.0%) — E&P recovering

OUR POSITIONING TODAY
- LONG core (E&P/Refining):  +1.01%  — OGDC 1.66%, PPL 1.57%, ATRL 1.83%
- SHORT (Cement DGKC+KOHC): -0.06%  — KOHC working (-1.19%), DGKC against us (0.66%)
- BANKS (deferred trade): +0.72%  — bounce arrived without us; relief is local-led, foreign FIPI still net-selling 4 days
- AVOID list: +0.53%  — bounced but only recovers fraction of yesterday's -2.5%

WHAT YESTERDAY DID — POST-MORTEM
- KSE-100 closed 161,805.02 (-2.29% from Friday)
- FIPI day 4 of foreign net-selling: foreign -PKR 0.55mn, banks -USD 1.01M
- Foreign sell-streak now 4 days, cumulative -1.94 PKR mn
- All ex-div names took their dividend cut
- Predictor guards correctly downgraded HBL, KOHC, LUCK -> AVOID; LOTCHEM -> WATCH (regime detection working)

TODAY'S PRIORITIES
1. DO NOT chase the bank bounce. Wait for foreign FIPI to FLIP
   (one day of foreign net-buying +USD 0.5M or more). Today's
   FIPI prints after close ~18:00 PKT. If positive, deploy 20%
   into MCB/UBL/MEBL tomorrow open. If still negative (5d streak),
   bounce is dead-cat and we re-enter cement shorts harder.

2. CEMENT SHORTS — tighten:
   - KOHC at 78.52 — short profitable, day high 81.94. New buy-stop at 80.50 (3% above current).
   - DGKC at 175.8 — bounced. New buy-stop at
     178.50 (just above yesterday's high). If breaks, COVER.
     We're break-even to slight loss on DGKC; KOHC carries.

3. LONG CORE — hold, do not add:
   - OGDC at 315.52 — entry yesterday ~314, stop 297. Hold. Don't chase if it pushes >320.
   - PPL, POL, MARI: hold. ATRL at 890.0 is +2% today, do not add at the top of the bounce.

4. AVOID list — STILL AVOID:
   - KEL, HUBC, EPCL, KAPCO, NPL all bouncing but the structural
     issues (circular debt, leverage) are unchanged. Avoid.

5. CASH FLOOR — 50% until foreign FIPI flip
   - Deployment plan if FIPI flips positive tonight:
       +20% into Banks (MCB / UBL / MEBL split equal)
       +10% into E&P add-ons (PPL, OGDC) if pullback to entry
       -10% from cement shorts (cover DGKC, hold KOHC)
   - If FIPI extends to 5d net-sell: drop to 65% cash, add to
     cement short (one more 2% in MLCF), no bank deployment.

MACRO BACKDROP (fresh)
- Brent USD 110.44000244140625/bbl (still elevated, defensive E&P tailwind)
- WTI USD 103.5199966430664/bbl
- USDPKR 278.368896484375 (stable)
- Gold USD 4552.60009765625, Copper USD 6.264500141143799
- KSE-100 EOD May 18: 162820.41 (-2.29% from Fri)

OPS NOTES
- Workflow sentinel landed (commit d7b282c). This file is
  flagged human_override=True so the scheduled rule-based v2
  call will park its output as 2026-05-19_workflow_autorun.json
  rather than overwriting this analysis.
- Anthropic API key still invalid — LLM strategist + news
  scoring + intraday session workflow remain down. Health
  check correctly flags news_scoring SLA breach. User to rotate.
- Predictor guards (cb378eb) running clean in production:
  HBL -> AVOID, KOHC -> AVOID, LUCK -> AVOID, LOTCHEM -> WATCH.
